Preparation 0%
Phase 0 · Preparation
Materials & Safety
Tick off every item before you begin. Never proceed without safety equipment.
Always wear nitrile gloves and eye protection when handling sodium hydroxide. It causes severe chemical burns. Work in a well-ventilated area — naphtha is highly flammable.
Phase 0 · Preparation
Naphtha Evaporation Test
Mandatory. Impure naphtha contaminates your product. Do not skip.
Do not use naphtha that fails this test. Discard and source a new batch.
    Use a small mirror — residue is far easier to spot than on clear glass. Valid naphtha: CAS 64742-49-0 or CAS 68410-97-9. Single-isomer n-heptane >99%: CAS 142-82-5. Mixed-isomer heptane is not recommended.
    Phase 1 · Acidification
    Choose Acidification Method
    Vinegar (acetic acid) drops pH to 2–3, converting freebase alkaloids to their water-soluble acetate salt.
    ❄️
    Freeze / Thaw ×3
    Recommended. 2 pulls needed. 80–95% white crystals. Takes 3+ days.
    🧪
    72 hr Vinegar Soak
    Simpler. 3 pulls needed. 70–85% white crystals. 72–96 hours.
    Freeze/thaw is recommended — consistently produces more white crystals and requires only 2 solvent pulls instead of 3.
    Heating the mixture (50–60°C) is not recommended — it releases plant impurities that significantly increase yellow product.
    Example yields — 100g material @ 1% concentration
    MethodPull 1Pull 2Pull 3Total
    Freeze/Thaw ×30.750g0.220g0.030g1.00g
    72hr Soak0.250g0.600g0.175g1.00g
    Phase 1 · Acidification
    Acidification: Freeze / Thaw ×3
    Set your material weight below — all reagent amounts and pull volumes scale automatically.

    Theoretical yield1.000 g
      Phase 2 · Base Preparation
      Preparing the Alkaline Base
      Sodium hydroxide raises pH to 12–13, converting the alkaloid salt back to freebase form — soluble in non-polar solvents.
      Add NaOH slowly to water — never water to NaOH. The reaction releases intense heat. Stir continuously. Gloves and eye protection required throughout.
      ⚙ Scaled Amounts
      Water1000 mL
      NaOH110 g
      Salt (NaCl)200 gAdd while solution is still hot
        The salt (NaCl) is a salting-out agent — it pushes alkaloids out of the aqueous layer and into the naphtha. Don't skip it.
        Phase 2 · Base Preparation
        Combining & Temperature Control
        Temperature at pull time is the single biggest factor in crystal quality. Target exactly 20°C.
        The base solution must be completely cool before you add the bark mixture. Do not rush this step.
          Naphtha pulls: target 20°C exactly.  |  n-Heptane pulls: 35–40°C. Mixed-isomer heptane is not recommended. Even 5°C above target noticeably increases yellow impurities.
          Leave your NaOH/salt/water solution in a fridge overnight before adding the bark mix — it makes hitting 20°C much easier. A small amount of hot water can fine-tune the temperature upward.
          Phase 3 · Solvent Pulls
          Naphtha Pulls
          Naphtha dissolves and extracts the freebase alkaloids from the alkaline solution.
          Naphtha is highly flammable. No open flames or sparks. Work in a ventilated area only.
          ⚙ Pull Volumes & Estimated Yields
          PullSolventEst. YieldCumulative
          Expected Total
          Recovery Rate
            Mark the liquid level with tape before adding naphtha — this lets you see clearly when the full naphtha layer has risen to the top after separation.
            Phase 4 · Crystal Recovery
            Choose Recovery Method
            How you recover crystals from naphtha affects final purity and colour.
            🧊
            Freeze Precipitation
            Recommended. More white crystals. 6–12 hrs in freezer.
            💨
            Evaporation
            Fan-dry naphtha away. Slightly more yellow. 24–48 hrs.
            Freeze precipitation produces a higher proportion of pristine white crystals compared to evaporation.
            Estimated Crystal Quality
            ⬜ White: 90% 🟡 Yellow: 10%
            Dry ice in a small cooler under your covered dish gives dramatically faster freeze precipitation — often under an hour.
            Phase 4 · Crystal Recovery
            Freeze Precipitation
            Isolating and harvesting your crystals.
              Complete
              Extraction Complete
              Your crystals are ready.
